data.title: Aave and Blockdaemon partner to boost institutional access to DeFi services
data.description: Blockdaemon, a leading institutional staking service, and Aave Labs, a core contributor to the Aave protocol, have entered into a strategic partnership to expand institutional access to decentralized finance (DeFi) opportunities. Aave Labs and Blockdaemon are committed to providing institutional-grade access to the Aave DeFi market, leveraging Blockdaemon's Earn Stack and Aave Vaults to achieve this goal.

data.title: "Bitcoin Jesus" Roger Ver to Pay $48 Million to Reach Deferred Prosecution Agreement with the US Department of Justice
data.description: According to the New York Times, early Bitcoin investor Roger Ver ("Bitcoin Jesus") has reached a deferred prosecution agreement (DPA) with the US Department of Justice, agreeing to pay approximately $48 million to settle tax fraud and tax evasion charges. The charges will be dismissed if the terms are met. Ver was indicted in 2024 for failing to pay approximately $48 million in taxes on crypto assets, and this year he hired a team of lawyers and lobbyists who previously defended Trump. Documents also indicate that he paid Roger Stone $600,000 to seek tax changes. The case has not yet been brought to court, and details are subject to change.

data.title: SEC issues new guidance allowing IPO companies to file registration documents without an offering price during the government shutdown
data.description: According to Bloomberg, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has issued new guidance allowing companies planning IPOs to submit registration documents without specifying a specific offering price during the government shutdown, aiming to alleviate regulatory bottlenecks caused by the approval process. The SEC emphasized that while this measure streamlines the process, it may still raise questions about or require revisions to companies' financial reports.

data.title: Mexico delays vote on 50% tariffs for Chinese imports
data.description:
Mexico’s Congress has postponed voting on proposed tariffs of up to 50% on Chinese and other Asian imports until late November.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/mexico-delays-vote-on-50-tariffs-for-china/

data.title: Ethereum Expected to Rebound After Recent Market Correction
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Tom Lee recently shared insights from Mark Newton, an analyst at his fund, regarding Ethereum's market performance. Newton suggests that the recent downturn in the crypto market is not a cause for concern, predicting that Ethereum will bottom out in the next one to two days before rising again to reach $5,500. From September 25 to October 7, Ethereum experienced a strong technical rally, followed by a minor three-wave correction, which is expected to conclude by October 11, leading to a renewed upward trend.

Currently, Ethereum has retraced 50% of its recent gains but may dip further to the $4,200-$4,220 range, which Newton identifies as a strong support zone before the cryptocurrency resumes its upward movement.

It is important to note that while Newton has previously made accurate predictions about Ethereum's trajectory, on September 16, he anticipated that Ethereum would not fall below its previous low of $4,233 and might drop to $4,418 or $4,375 over the weekend, suggesting a buying opportunity during this weakness. However, Ethereum briefly dipped to $4,054 shortly thereafter.

data.title: Major Event Management Platform Raises $2M To Expand Stablecoin Payments Across Entertainment Industry
data.description:
As the stablecoin sector sees global momentum grow, white label event management infrastructure provider Rhuna has raised $2 million in seed funding to expand stablecoin payments in the entertainment industry. Related Reading: BNB Chain Memecoin Season? 70% Of Investors In Profit As Four. Meme Surpasses Pump. Fun Rhuna Raises $2 Million In Seed Round On Thursday, Romania-based infrastructure platform Rhuna announced the completion of a $2 million seed round, led by Aptos Labs, to continue providing on-chain payments, access, identity, and rewards infrastructure for the entertainment industry
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/major-event-management-platform-raises-2m-to-expand-stablecoin-payments-across-entertainment-industry/

data.title: Yi He: The Core of MEME Lies in Fairness, Opportunity, and Shared Consensus, True Building Means Solving User Problems
data.description: Binance Co-founder Yi He published a long post on Binance Square, sharing her evolving perspective on MEME culture. She admitted that she once viewed MEME tokens as conspiracy-driven schemes wrapped in subcultural aesthetics, believing that only projects that take responsibility for their holders were on the right path. However, as the industry developed, she also witnessed some projects treating Binance as their “final liquidity exit,"noting that “when facing professional traders, retail investors are defenseless.”Yi He said she had long struggled with the question of how both professional traders and retail users could profit, until she realized that most users are not seeking to win forever, they simply want fairness. In her view, the true essence of MEME lies in “that bit of fairness, opportunity, and shared consensus, built grain by grain like a sandcastle.” “I used to oppose MEMEs, then tried to understand them—and in the end, I became one. The clown is me,” she wrote. At the same time, Yi He reminded investors to stay cautious, emphasizing that MEMEs have no responsible teams, no long-term value foundation, and are often manipulated by organized groups. She clarified that neither Binance nor herself has endorsed any MEME tokens. Yi He further noted that as a founder who believes in value investing and long-termism, she sees true entrepreneurship as solving real user problems through sustainable business models. She added that YZi Labs plans to allocate $1 billion to support ecosystem builders and developers—an effort she described as “what real business competition looks like.”

data.title: Bankr Faces Suspension on Telegram and X Platforms
data.description: According to BlockBeats, AI agency platform Bankr has been suspended from Telegram due to unspecified violations of the platform's terms of service. The platform has encountered similar issues on X, formerly known as Twitter, leading to its suspension there as well. Bankr's co-founder, Deployer, stated that they are currently in communication with both Telegram and Twitter teams to resolve the situation.

data.title: U.S. Senate Proposal Targets High-Risk DeFi Protocols and Crypto Regulations
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Cointelegraph reports that Democratic members of the U.S. Senate Banking Committee have proposed granting the Treasury Department the authority to list high-risk DeFi protocols as restricted, making their use a criminal offense. The proposal also calls for implementing Know Your Customer (KYC) rules on crypto application frontends, including non-custodial wallets, and reducing legal protections for crypto developers.

Crypto attorney Jake Chervinsky criticized the proposal, stating, "This is terrible. It is not regulating cryptocurrency; it is banning it." Zunera Mazhar, Vice President of Government and Policy Affairs at the Chamber of Digital Commerce, described the measures as harsh and ineffective, warning that they could lead to an exodus of innovation rather than addressing actual risks.

data.title: Grayscale Stakes 857,600 Ethereum Worth $3.83B As Institutional Confidence Grows
data.description:
Ethereum is trading at critical levels after a period of heightened volatility that has left traders and investors on edge. The price has been swinging between key resistance and support zones, reflecting a market torn between optimism for another leg higher and caution over potential short-term corrections. While sentiment remains divided, on-chain data paints a more confident picture behind the scenes. Related Reading: Short-Term Holder Supply Rises By 559K Bitcoin – New Buyers Flood the Market According to recent reports, large holders and institutions continue to accumulate ETH, reinforcing the idea that the current market uncertainty may be viewed by many as an opportunity rather than a threat
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/ethereum/grayscale-stakes-857600-ethereum-worth-3-83b-as-institutional-confidence-grows/

data.title: Bitdeer Technologies Expands Mining Operations Amid Slowing Demand
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Bitdeer Technologies Group, a Nasdaq-listed company founded by cryptocurrency veteran Jihan Wu, is undergoing a strategic transformation by significantly expanding its mining operations in response to slowing buyer demand. As of August 2025, the company has quadrupled its self-operated mining capacity compared to the same period last year.

Bitdeer aims to continue its expansion with the goal of becoming one of the top five cryptocurrency miners globally. This strategic move positions the company in direct competition with its own clients.

data.title: SEC Offers Temporary Guidance for IPOs Amid Government Shutdown
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has issued temporary guidance for companies seeking to launch initial public offerings (IPOs) during the ongoing government shutdown. The guidance allows issuers to submit registration documents without specifying the exact offering price, with the documents automatically becoming effective after 20 days. However, companies may still be required to address inquiries or make revisions subsequently.

The shutdown has resulted in approximately 90% of SEC staff being furloughed, causing a halt in IPO reviews. Companies such as Navan, Andersen Group, and cryptocurrency custody firm Bitgo, which have already filed for IPOs, can proceed with their marketing efforts under this new guidance.
